headline: Digital Court System Handles 280,000 Cases Online
context: Malaysia e-Court processed 280,000 cases through digital filing and virtual hearings representing 35% of total proceedings.

cards.big: E-Court handled 280,000 cases online reaching 35% of total proceedings
cards.sub: Digital transformation of judiciary accelerated by pandemic

cards.big: Virtual hearings reduced average case time from 18 to 12 months
cards.sub: Lawyers save 2.8 million hours annually in travel and waiting

cards.big: 65% of Magistrate Court cases filed digitally but High Court only 28%
cards.sub: Complex cases and judicial preference limit upper court digitization

cards.big: Rural courts in 8 states lack bandwidth for reliable video hearings
cards.sub: 12 courtrooms upgraded with RM8 million but 45 more need connectivity

cards.big: Court digitization tests whether justice system technology reaches all courts equally
280,000 cases handled online. Digital courts succeed when access to justice improves, not just efficiency.
